items.risk: Joint & several liability for breach-caused harm — broad, no cap
Unanimous consent for Additional Capital — creates deadlock risk if one party refuses
Asymmetric capital economics: no interest on contributions (cl 22), but interest IS payable on Member advances at majority-set rate (cl 20)
Confidentiality survives only 1 year after termination — short for a JV
Force Majeure ridiculously broad — 'any other unforeseen and uncontrollable event'
No Reserved Matters / supermajority list — only 'majority vote of Members' for major issues
Involuntary Withdrawal triggers indemnity by the defaulting Member — broad, no cap
No dilution formula for Members who don't fund additional capital
Mediation rules unspecified — 'statutory rules of mediation' — vague
Severability with judicial reformation — court may rewrite, not just strike
Bankruptcy / Operation of Law triggers Involuntary Withdrawal
Conflict-of-interest disclosure with majority consent only

qualitative_tone: should reference operator/non-operator dynamics (but this JV is symmetrical, no operator), capital calls, deadlock, reserved matters
